description: China's largest agricultural product processing enterprise, a global leading grain, oil and food enterprise

traditional_rating.limitation: Comprehensive rating cannot accurately identify supply chain transmission risks

analysis.method: Enterprise Environmental Report Review + Production Facility Monitoring
key_findings: Committed to achieving carbon peak by 2030
Environmental impact of midstream processing is relatively controllable
analysis.conclusion: As a midstream processor, COFCO has good environmental performance in its own production processes, but needs to focus on the environmental compliance of upstream raw material procurement

positive_actions: Established green supply chain management system

analysis.method: Supply Chain Due Diligence + Public Opinion Monitoring
analysis.key_concern: Labor issues of Malaysian palm oil supplier FGV may affect COFCO's compliance

key_events.event: U.S. CBP listed FGV on the Forced Labor Import Ban List
key_events.impact: As a downstream customer of FGV, COFCO faces supply chain compliance review pressure

key_events.event: EU proposed draft of the Due Diligence Directive
key_events.impact: Requires companies to take responsibility for human rights and environmental risks in their supply chains

key_events.event: EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) officially came into effect
key_events.impact: Must prove that palm oil raw materials have no deforestation risk, compliance to be completed by December 30, 2024

key_events.event: FGV submitted remediation petition to U.S. CBP
key_events.impact: If remediation is successful, it will reduce COFCO's supply chain risk; continued monitoring is still required

risk_mitigation: Increasing diversification of palm oil suppliers
Maintaining communication with FGV and supervising remediation progress

highlights: State-owned enterprise with standardized governance structure

suppliers.name: FGV Holdings Berhad
suppliers.note: Affected by U.S. CBP ban, currently under remediation

risk_transmission_path: FGV labor issues → U.S. market ban → COFCO procurement compliance risk
FGV potential deforestation risk → EU EUDR requirements → COFCO export restrictions

markets.risk: If upstream FGV fails review, exports may be blocked
Must prove raw materials are not from FGV during the ban period

markets.regulation: Relatively lenient, but consumer ESG awareness is increasing

downstream.impact_analysis: If FGV issues persist, COFCO may face: 1) Export restrictions to Europe and US markets; 2) Increased cost of replacing suppliers; 3) Damage to brand image

short_term: Increase procurement ratio from low-risk suppliers such as IOI
Prepare EUDR compliance documents and traceability proof
long_term: Invest in domestic oil crop planting bases
Participate in industry sustainable standard setting (such as RSPO certification)

requirements: Prove palm oil raw materials have no deforestation risk
Provide geographic location data and satellite imagery proof
EUDR.progress: Supplier audits initiated, collecting satellite data

US_CBP.risk: If procurement from FGV continues, may face U.S. market access issues
US_CBP.action: Suspended direct procurement from FGV, awaiting remediation results

vs_traditional_rating: Traditional rating: MSCI gave BBB, cannot identify supply chain risks
GreenLink rating: Social risk 45 points, accurately identifies transmission risk from FGV
real_time_monitoring: July 2024 FGV submitted remediation petition → GreenLink system updated in real time
Traditional ratings updated annually with lagging response
actionable_insights: Clearly states: Need to diversify suppliers and reduce dependence on FGV
Provides compliance report templates to assist EUDR declaration

immediate: Communicate supply chain remediation progress with European and American clients
medium_term: Reduce FGV procurement ratio from current 30% to below 10%
Increase procurement from RSPO certified suppliers such as IOI
long_term: Invest in building domestic oil bases to achieve raw material self-control
Develop intelligent traceability system using blockchain + satellite remote sensing
Become an industry benchmark for sustainable supply chains
